content:
type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please add tests for dry_run in Tests/test_image.py. Spec for dry_run feature: Image.save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params) - a new keyword-only-ish param 'dry_run' (default False) on Image.save(). When dry_run=True: the method still resolves format/filename, calls _ensure_mutable(), sets up encoderinfo/encoderconfig, selects save_handler (SAVE or SAVE_ALL), and INVOKES the save_handler against a throwaway in-memory buffer (io.BytesIO()) instead of opening/writing the real file at 'fp' -- so any exceptions the encoder would raise (e.g. unsupported mode for format) still raise normally. No file is created/opened/removed on disk when dry_run=True, and the real fp is never touched. Returns None as usual. Suggested tests: 1) save(tmp_path/'out.png', dry_run=True) then assert the file does NOT exist at that path. 2) dry_run=True with a normal save afterwards (dry_run=False) still produces a valid file with correct content, to show dry_run didn't corrupt any state (encoderinfo cleaned up in finally). 3) dry_run=True still raises for an incompatible mode/format combo (e.g. saving CMYK to a format that doesn't support it, or similar existing error case) -- reuse an existing 'unsupported mode' test scenario if one exists in the file. 4) dry_run=True works when fp is a filename string, not just file object. Place tests near existing test_save / test_fp tests in Tests/test_image.py.

Place tests near existing test_save / test_fp tests in Tests/test_image.py.
